title: rapanea urceolata
intro: Rapanea urceolata, also known as the Yellow Wood or Yellow Wood Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. The plant is native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in the Hawaiian Islands. Rapanea urceolata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function.
